hero.description: Empowering the next generation of scientists, researchers, and innovators through world-class laboratories, research-driven academics, and hands-on scientific training.

vision.title: Vision
vision.label: Our Purpose
vision.text: To emerge as a premier center of scientific learning and research, fostering a culture of innovation that addresses global challenges through fundamental and applied sciences.

mission.title: Mission
mission.label: Our Goal
points: Deliver rigorous, concept-driven academic programs in core scientific disciplines.
Promote interdisciplinary research that connects basic sciences with real-world industry applications.
Equip students with cutting-edge analytical, mathematical, and laboratory skills.
Collaborate with leading scientific organizations and biotech industries to ensure career readiness.

programmes.subtitle: Academic Offerings
programmes.description: Explore our specialized undergraduate, postgraduate, and doctoral programs in Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and Biotechnology.

bsc.title: Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.)
bsc.eligibility: 10+2 (Science) with minimum 50% marks from a recognized board
bsc.description: The B.Sc. program builds a robust foundation in core sciences (Mathematics and Biology), preparing students for careers in research, healthcare, ed-tech, and analytics.
bsc.metaTitle: Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) | Basic & Applied Sciences | CPU
bsc.metaDescription: Pursue a 3-year B.Sc. degree at Career Point University. Specialize in Maths or Biology with intensive laboratory training and industry-aligned curriculum.

hero.description: Master the fundamentals of nature, matter, and mathematics. Gain hands-on laboratory experience and prepare for a high-impact scientific career.

overview.subtitle: What is the B.Sc. Programme?
paragraphs: The B.Sc. programme at Career Point University provides a comprehensive education in basic sciences, combining rigorous classroom theory with extensive laboratory practice.
Students develop strong analytical, mathematical, and problem-solving skills, making them highly sought after by R&D sectors, pharmaceuticals, ed-tech, and government scientific agencies.

scope.body: A B.Sc. degree opens doors to diverse and lucrative career paths. Graduates can pursue roles as lab technicians, research assistants, quality control analysts, data analysts, and environmental consultants. Furthermore, it serves as the essential stepping stone for higher education (M.Sc., Ph.D.) and forms a strong base for competitive exams in government scientific sectors like DRDO, ISRO, and forensic laboratories.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in the world of science

curriculum.subtitle: A scientifically structured curriculum for innovators
curriculum.introNote: The B.Sc. curriculum is designed to impart foundational theoretical knowledge alongside advanced experimental and laboratory techniques.
curriculum.outroNote: Graduates are fully equipped to pursue Master's programs or enter the workforce as scientific analysts.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) program offers a balanced mix of theoretical instruction and practical laboratory sessions. Students must choose their core combinations at the beginning of the program: either the Mathematics Group (PCM - Physics, Chemistry, Maths) or the Biology Group (CBZ - Chemistry, Botany, Zoology).

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Mechanics/Diversity of Microbes, Inorganic Chemistry I, Differential Calculus/Cell Biology, English Communication, Lab Practice I. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Electricity & Magnetism/Plant Ecology, Organic Chemistry I, Integral Calculus/Genetics, Environmental Science, Lab Practice II. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Thermal Physics/Plant Anatomy, Physical Chemistry I, Real Analysis/Animal Physiology, Computer Applications in Science, Lab Practice III. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Optics/Plant Physiology, Organic Chemistry II, Algebra/Developmental Biology, Scientific Writing, Lab Practice IV. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-5
accordionSections.content: Quantum Mechanics/Molecular Biology, Spectroscopy, Matrices/Immunology, Discipline Specific Elective I, Lab Practice V. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-6
accordionSections.content: Solid State Physics/Biotechnology Basics, Biochemistry, Complex Analysis/Ecology, Minor Project / Dissertation, Lab Practice VI. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Departmental Elective
accordionSections.content: Elective options include: Material Science, Analytical Chemistry, Linear Programming, Applied Zoology, and Bioinformatics.

applySteps.subtitle: Simple 5-step process to join the B.Sc. programme

items.a: Candidates must have passed 10+2 from a recognized board with a minimum of 50% marks in the Science stream (PCM or PCB).
items.q: What is the eligibility criteria for B.Sc. admission?

items.a: B.Sc. is a 3-year (6 semester) programme. The tuition fee is ₹18,000 per semester. Additional charges include a laboratory fee of ₹3,000 per semester.
items.q: What is the duration and fee structure of the B.Sc. programme?

items.a: Students must choose a core group in the first year: either the Mathematics group (Physics, Chemistry, Maths) or the Biology group (Chemistry, Botany, Zoology).
items.q: Can I choose between Maths and Biology?

items.a: Group changes are generally only permitted within the first month of the first semester, subject to seat availability and academic approval.
items.q: Can I switch my specialization (e.g., from PCM to CBZ) later?

items.a: B.Sc. graduates find roles in pharmaceutical companies, chemical industries, clinical labs, ed-tech platforms, and as data analysts. We provide active campus placement support.
items.q: What kind of placements can I expect after completing B.Sc.?

items.a: Yes. Merit-based scholarships of up to 100% on tuition fees are available for students scoring above 90% in their 12th board exams.
items.q: Is there any scholarship available for B.Sc. students?

items.a: Yes, our central instrumentation lab has advanced equipment like spectrophotometers, centrifuges, and microscopes which are accessible to undergraduate students during practicals.
items.q: Are the laboratories equipped for advanced research?

items.a: Yes, B.Sc. is the foundational degree for pursuing M.Sc. in specialized fields, MCA, MBA, or even B.Ed. for a career in teaching.
items.q: Can I pursue higher studies after completing B.Sc.?

years.contentDesc: Focus on foundational scientific theories, basic mathematics, and strict laboratory safety protocols.

Final year dissertation, specialized electives, and hands-on work with advanced instrumentation.

bsc-biotech.title: B.Sc. Bio Technology
bsc-biotech.eligibility: 10+2 (Science/Biology) with minimum 50% marks from a recognized board
bsc-biotech.description: An advanced undergraduate program bridging biology with technology to develop innovations in healthcare, agriculture, and environmental sustainability.
bsc-biotech.metaTitle: B.Sc. Bio Technology | Basic & Applied Sciences | CPU
bsc-biotech.metaDescription: Pursue a 3-year B.Sc. Bio Technology degree at Career Point University. Master genetics, molecular biology, and bioinformatics with intensive lab training.

hero.description: Unlock the power of cellular and biomolecular processes. Prepare for high-tech careers in pharmaceuticals, genetics, and cutting-edge R&D.

overview.subtitle: What is the B.Sc. Bio Technology Programme?
paragraphs: The B.Sc. Bio Technology program is a highly specialized course that explores the intersection of biology and modern technology. It focuses on harnessing cellular and biomolecular processes to develop technologies and products that help improve our lives and the health of our planet.

Students receive rigorous training in core subjects like Microbiology, Biochemistry, and Recombinant DNA Technology, backed by extensive hands-on laboratory sessions. This ensures they are fully prepared for the rapidly growing biotech and pharma industries.

scope.body: The scope of Biotechnology is vast and continuously expanding. Graduates are in high demand across pharmaceutical companies, clinical research organizations (CROs), agricultural biotech firms, and forensic science laboratories. Career roles include Research Associate, Quality Control Analyst, Bio-informatician, and Clinical Data Manager. Furthermore, this degree is the perfect stepping stone for advanced studies (M.Sc., Ph.D.) globally.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in modern life sciences

curriculum.subtitle: An advanced curriculum designed for biotech excellence
curriculum.introNote: The B.Sc. Bio Technology curriculum provides an in-depth understanding of biological sciences integrated with modern technological applications.
curriculum.outroNote: Graduates are fully equipped with the practical lab skills required for immediate placement in the R&D and pharma sectors.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The B.Sc. Bio Technology program is deeply focused on life sciences and their technological applications. The curriculum progresses from basic biological and chemical principles in the first year to advanced molecular techniques, bioinformatics, and bioprocessing in the final year.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Cell Biology, General Microbiology, Basic Chemistry, Mathematics for Biologists, English Communication, Lab Practice I. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Biochemistry & Metabolism, Genetics, Biostatistics, Environmental Science, Lab Practice II. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Molecular Biology, Plant Physiology, Immunology, Introduction to Computers, Lab Practice III. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Recombinant DNA Technology, Animal Physiology, Industrial Microbiology, Scientific Writing, Lab Practice IV. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-5
accordionSections.content: Bioprocess Engineering, Bioinformatics, Environmental Biotechnology, Discipline Specific Elective I, Lab Practice V. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-6
accordionSections.content: Genomics & Proteomics, Medical Biotechnology, Discipline Specific Elective II, Industrial Project / Dissertation. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Departmental Elective
accordionSections.content: Elective options include: Food Biotechnology, Nanotechnology, Clinical Research & Trials, and Agricultural Biotechnology.

items.a: Our students are placed in top pharmaceutical companies, biotech firms, clinical research organizations, and food tech companies as Research Analysts, QC Executives, and Lab Technicians.

msc.title: Master of Science (M.Sc.)
msc.eligibility: B.Sc. in relevant discipline with minimum 50% marks from a recognized university
msc.description: An intensive postgraduate program designed to create subject matter experts, elite researchers, and industry-ready scientists across multiple disciplines.
msc.metaTitle: Master of Science (M.Sc.) | Basic & Applied Sciences | CPU
msc.metaDescription: Pursue a 2-year M.Sc. degree at Career Point University. Specialize in Physics, Chemistry, Maths, Zoology, Botany, or Environmental Science with a focus on advanced R&D.

hero.description: Deepen your scientific expertise. Engage in high-level experimental research, mathematical modeling, and prepare for elite roles in academia and global R&D.

overview.subtitle: What is the M.Sc. Programme?
paragraphs: The Master of Science (M.Sc.) is a rigorous two-year, four-semester programme built for students who want to push the boundaries of known science. It moves beyond standard textbook learning to focus on complex theoretical frameworks, mathematical modeling, and high-level laboratory research.

Whether you are aiming to crack the CSIR-NET/JRF for a Ph.D., preparing for roles in the pharmaceutical industry, or looking to join space and defense research organizations, the M.Sc. programme provides the intellectual and experimental depth required to succeed.

scope.body: An M.Sc. degree signifies a high level of academic and experimental maturity. Graduates are highly sought after as R&D Scientists, Quality Assurance Managers, Data Analysts, Subject Matter Experts, and College Professors. Furthermore, the specialized knowledge gained during this programme acts as a massive catalyst for students appearing in the UPSC Civil Services Main Examination (Science Optionals) and direct scientific recruitment drives by DRDO, ISRO, and BARC.
scope.subtitle: Elevating your scientific and professional stature

curriculum.subtitle: A master-level curriculum focusing on advanced theory and research
curriculum.introNote: The M.Sc. programme is characterized by advanced computational modeling, intensive laboratory protocols, and a mandatory research dissertation.
curriculum.outroNote: Graduates leave not just with a degree, but with published scientific research and specialized expertise in their chosen domain.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The Master of Science (M.Sc.) program offers diverse specializations. The first year focuses on establishing strong advanced theoretical frameworks and experimental techniques. The second year allows students to dive deep into specialized electives (e.g., Astrophysics, Organic Synthesis, Applied Mathematics) and culminates in an independent research dissertation.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Core Subjects: Advanced Theoretical Science I, Computational Methods, Analytical Techniques, Core Lab Practice I. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Core Subjects: Advanced Theoretical Science II, Research Methodology, Statistics for Sciences, Core Lab Practice II, Seminar Presentation. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Advanced Studies: Specialization Paper I, Specialization Paper II, Advanced Lab Practice III, Synopsis Submission for Dissertation. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Research & Culmination: Master's Dissertation (Research, Writing, and Defense), Viva-Voce, Specialization Paper III. Total Credits: 22

items.a: Candidates must have completed their B.Sc. in a relevant discipline from a recognized university with a minimum of 50% marks.
items.q: What is the eligibility criteria for M.Sc. admission?

items.a: M.Sc. is a 2-year (4 semester) postgraduate programme.
items.q: What is the duration of the M.Sc. programme?

items.a: We currently offer M.Sc. in Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Zoology, Botany, and Environmental Science.
items.q: What subjects can I specialize in?

items.a: Yes, a final semester research dissertation (based on laboratory or computational research) is a mandatory requirement to complete the M.Sc. degree.
items.q: Is a research dissertation or lab project compulsory?

items.a: Our curriculum is heavily aligned with the CSIR-NET and GATE syllabi, and we provide special guidance sessions to help students clear these exams for academic and PSU careers.
items.q: Does this course help in preparing for CSIR-NET or GATE?

items.a: Yes, completing an M.Sc. with good grades and clearing CSIR-NET/JRF makes you eligible to apply directly for our Ph.D. scientific research programs.
items.q: Can I pursue a Ph.D. immediately after completing this M.Sc.?

The second year focuses on your chosen electives and culminates in the execution, writing, and defense of your Master's Research Thesis.

community.label: Community
description: The School of Basic & Applied Sciences is a thriving ecosystem of curiosity. Our community consists of brilliant minds dedicated to unraveling the mysteries of the universe, from complex mathematical proofs to genetic sequencing.

From national-level science exhibitions and hackathons to coding clubs and environmental drives, our students are constantly engaged in activities that broaden their scientific horizons and societal impact.

list.title: Central Instrumentation Facility
list.desc: Equipped with high-end tools like HPLC, FTIR, and UV-Vis Spectrophotometers for advanced chemical and biological analysis.

list.title: High-Performance Computing Lab
list.desc: Dedicated computational clusters for complex mathematical modeling, simulations, and computational physics.

list.title: Modern Bioscience & Tech Labs
list.desc: Featuring bio-safety cabinets, incubators, and genetic testing equipment for life sciences students.

list.title: Smart Lecture Halls
list.desc: Digitally enabled classrooms designed for interactive science learning and remote guest lectures.

list.name: Aditi Sharma
list.text: The lab facilities here are at par with industry standards. The practical exposure I got directly helped me secure my placement in R&D.

list.name: Vikram Singh
list.text: CPU's faculty didn't just teach us from books; they involved us in their live research projects. It completely changed my perspective on Physics.

list.name: Siddharth Jain
list.text: The rigorous mathematical training and computational skills I acquired here paved my way into the thriving field of Data Science.
